accepted_findings.message_regex: Detected possible user input going into a `path\.join` or `path\.resolve`
accepted_findings.reason: Flagged joins/resolves are in helper/config/build paths or are protected by validation and boundary checks (resolveSafePath, segment sanitization, controlled platform IDs).

accepted_findings.message_regex: Possible writing outside of the destination
accepted_findings.reason: The file path is derived through safe path resolution and constrained to event file directories; Semgrep cannot follow custom guard logic.
Controller uses PreviewRenderer.render helper, not Express template rendering with user-selected view names.
Regex creation is intentionally wrapped with source-length and pattern safety guards; usage is controlled and not exposed as arbitrary attacker input regex execution.

accepted_findings.message_regex: Possibility of prototype polluting function detected
accepted_findings.reason: Looped objects are schema/config controlled and not merged from untrusted prototype-bearing payloads; no unsafe Object.assign/deep-merge on attacker-controlled objects.

accepted_findings.message_regex: Detected string concatenation with a non-literal variable in a util\.format / console\.log function
accepted_findings.reason: Findings are informational logging patterns; not a security boundary and no attacker-controlled format strings are executed as code.
Upgrade headers are required for websocket support; backend upstream is fixed and not attacker-selectable.
Host forwarding is intentional behind controlled reverse proxy routing; upstream target is fixed and not derived from user input.
Navigation URLs come from platform workflow configuration and login endpoints, not arbitrary user-supplied URLs in a public request context.
Matched value is LinkedIn schema identifier text, not a credential.
File contains UI color/token constants; no secret material is present.
Matches are documentation/example placeholders used for encryption docs, not real API keys.
